title: Orpheus and Eurydice
summary: The repository XML is a copy of the 1993 University of Michigan item HenOrpheus, OTA U*-243-A. Its line text descends from the surviving 1984 Older Scottish Textual Archive / Oxford Text Archive COCOA payload now cataloged as ota:2231 and formerly held in OTA folder 0243: after headings and markup are removed, 632 of 633 normalized verse lines match, with only line 98 changing from BUS to buss. OTA's header credits A. J. Aitken, P. Bratley, and N. Hamilton-Smith with creating the machine-readable version, cites G. Gregory Smith's 1908 volume III pp. 26-64 as its printed base, and warns that variants may be included. Smith prints a Chepman-Myllar stream with 461 surviving lines from an imperfect unique copy, a 578-line Asloan stream mediated by Professor Biilbring's working transcript, and the 633-line Bannatyne stream. Comparison shows that the OTA text is Chepman-dominant, supplies the lost Chepman lines 59-175 mainly from Asloan with local eclectic readings, and takes Bannatyne-only additions at 509-514, 547-550, and 571-615. U-M then converted the uppercase COCOA text into structured mixed-case XML, supplied navigation divisions and Smith's Bannatyne-stream page milestones 66-87, and omitted Smith's editorial matter. This record keeps the medieval work, witnesses, transcript intermediary, scholarly editions, two electronic stages, repository artifact, and source objects distinct.

agents.name: University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative
Robert Henryson
agents.dates: active late fifteenth century; died before or by 1506?
notes: Exact life dates remain uncertain; the repository header's 1430?-1506? formula is not treated as established fact.

agents.name: George Stevenson
notes: Smith credits Stevenson with transcribing and collating the edition's texts.

agents.name: Professor Biilbring of Bonn
notes: Name and affiliation are recorded in Smith's spelling; no unverified expansion of the identity is supplied here.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for HenOrpheus
entities.description: The pinned XML artifact consumed by this repository and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: File size is 37,181 bytes at the reviewed commit.
The XML contains two DIV1, six supplied DIV2, 57 LG, 633 consecutively numbered L, and 22 PB elements.

entities.label: University of Michigan electronic item HenOrpheus, OTA U*-243-A
entities.description: A public 1993 electronic edition structured for U-M's Humanities Text Initiative. Its header names OTA reference U*-243-A and Smith volume III, pp. 66-87. Exact comparison establishes that its 633 verse lines are a mixed-case, structured transformation of the 1984 OSTA/OTA COCOA payload now cataloged as ota:2231, with one normalized line-level difference and added navigation and page structures.
notes: Front matter, back matter, and Smith's editorial notes are explicitly omitted.
The six supplied line-range divisions are electronic navigation units rather than witness structure.

The OTA header's 2000 revision says the Orpheus text formerly belonged to folder 0243 before receiving its own item 2231; this explains the U*-243-A identifier without conflating the 1984 and 1993 encodings.

entities.label: Older Scottish Textual Archive / Oxford Text Archive COCOA text, ota:2231
entities.description: The surviving complete 633-line uppercase COCOA text prepared for the Older Scottish Textual Archive and the Dictionary of the Older Scottish Tongue, deposited with OTA no later than 1984-05-01. Its TEI catalog header names Smith volume III pp. 26-64 as the printed source, describes Chepman-Myllar as the apparent base, and warns that variants may be included. Direct comparison confirms a Chepman-dominant reconstructed stream with Asloan-dominant supply for the unique print copy's lost lines 59-175, Bannatyne-only additions, and local eclectic readings.

date_statements.label: deposit and latest possible digital creation

notes: Harry D. Watson and the Dictionary of the Older Scottish Tongue are named as depositors; the header separately assigns creation of the machine-readable version to Aitken, Bratley, and Hamilton-Smith.
The header says the text formed part of the Older Scottish Textual Archive and was intended to ensure complete quotation coverage for DOST.

A 2000 revision records that this text moved from folder 0243 to item 2231. It also reports a separate unavailable copy, orpheus2.2231, lacking lines 59-175; that incomplete copy is not the cached or modeled payload.
The available payload retains COCOA page and folio tags at sparse source locations and encodes the full 633-line work.

entities.label: G. Gregory Smith, The Poems of Robert Henryson, volume III (1908)
entities.description: Scottish Text Society 58, volume III overall and text volume II. Smith prints the surviving Chepman-Myllar, Asloan-transcript, and Bannatyne Orpheus streams separately and supplies both the base presentation used by the OSTA/OTA encoding and the Bannatyne page sequence later added by U-M.

bibliographic.citation: The Poems of Robert Henryson, ed. G. Gregory Smith, vol. III, Text vol. II, Scottish Text Society 58 (Edinburgh and London: William Blackwood and Sons, 1908).
bibliographic.title: The Poems of Robert Henryson
bibliographic.pages: Orpheus: Chepman-Myllar pp. 26-64 left; Asloan transcript pp. 27-65 right; Bannatyne pp. 66-87

notes: Smith thanks George Stevenson for transcribing and collating the texts.

The three streams contain 461 surviving, 578, and 633 lines respectively; the Chepman count reflects two lost leaves in the unique surviving copy, not proof that the original print intentionally omitted lines 59-175.
The Asloan stream is explicitly based on a working transcript lent by Professor Biilbring, not on Smith's direct access to the codex.

entities.label: G. Gregory Smith, The Poems of Robert Henryson, volume I (1914)
entities.description: The introduction, appendix, notes, glossary, and indexes volume of Smith's edition. It documents the three Orpheus sources, Biilbring transcript, length differences, Henryson attribution, and literary-source discussion omitted from the electronic item.

bibliographic.citation: The Poems of Robert Henryson, ed. G. Gregory Smith, vol. I, Introduction, Appendix, Notes, Index of Words and Glossary, General Index (Edinburgh and London: William Blackwood and Sons, 1914).
bibliographic.title: The Poems of Robert Henryson
bibliographic.pages: Orpheus introduction pp. xlix et seq.; notes pp. 53-58

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Smith volume III (1908)
entities.description: A 232-page public PDF derivative with OCR, scan metadata, and item metadata cached for source verification. The derivative jumps from printed page 36 to 38 and therefore omits at least printed page 37 of the Asloan stream; it is not a complete reproduction of Smith volume III.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, and scan-data XML

entities.label: Google Books public-domain scan of Smith volume III (1908)
entities.description: A separate full-view page-image scan that includes printed page 37, missing from the Internet Archive derivative. Google exposes a full-PDF route, but automated retrieval encountered an interactive challenge; only the exact page-37 image needed to close the source gap is locally cached, so no complete local Google Books copy is claimed.
entities.reproduction_format: Full-view page images with PDF and EPUB routes; local PNG of printed page 37

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Smith volume I (1914)
entities.description: A complete 360-page public PDF with OCR, scan metadata, and item metadata cached for source and critical-note verification.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, and scan-data XML

entities.label: Chepman and Myllar's 1508 print of Orpheus
entities.description: The first surviving publication, printed at Edinburgh in April 1508. The unique surviving copy is imperfect: the third and fourth leaves of its first gathering are lost, removing unified lines 59-175 and leaving 461 surviving lines. That physical lacuna is not treated as proof of an intentionally shorter printed version. The schema has no historical-print-witness class, so the print is represented as a scholarly-edition entity with that limitation explicit.

bibliographic.citation: Heir begynnis the traitie of Orpheus kyng and how he yeid to hewyn and to hel to seik his quene (Edinburgh: Walter Chepman and Androw Myllar, April 1508).
bibliographic.title: Heir begynnis the traitie of Orpheus kyng and how he yeid to hewyn and to hel to seik his quene

historical_designations: eighth tract in The Porteous of Noblenes and ten other rare tracts
holding.notes: The current precise shelfmark was not established from the reviewed public project pages; the surviving collection's leaves were separately mounted in the twentieth century.

notes: The tract's title advertises ‘ane othir ballad in the lattir end.’ That following lyric is The Want of Wise Men, a separate work not part of Orpheus.

The facsimile completely represents the published surviving-image sequence, including the unique copy's lacuna; it does not restore the two lost leaves or constitute a complete copy of the original printed tract.

entities.label: Complete 220-image public derivative of the Chepman and Myllar prints
entities.description: A complete 220-image DjVu derivative publicly served by Wikimedia Commons. Complete refers to the published facsimile sequence of the surviving collection, not the original printed objects: the unique Orpheus copy has lost two leaves carrying unified lines 59-175.

entities.label: Asloan manuscript, NLS MS 16500
entities.description: John Asloan's early-sixteenth-century anthology. Orpheus occupies fols. 247r-256v and preserves a 578-line version, including the 117-line section absent from Chepman-Myllar but lacking 55 Moralitas lines present in Bannatyne.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: 1513-1542 in the NLS catalog; Orpheus often dated c. 1515-1530

notes: The complete digital object represents the surviving manuscript; historical mutilation elsewhere in the codex is not repaired by digitization.

entities.label: Professor Biilbring's working transcript of the Asloan Orpheus
entities.description: A modern working transcript made while the Asloan manuscript was deposited at the British Museum and lent to Smith by Professor Biilbring of Bonn. It is an intermediary transcription, not an independent medieval witness; the controlled manuscript-witness class is used because the schema has no separate working-transcript type.
notes: No current shelfmark, institutional location, or public reproduction for the transcript was found.

entities.label: NLS complete digital surrogate of the Asloan manuscript
entities.description: The 628-canvas NLS IIIF object for the full surviving manuscript. Orpheus occupies canvases 507-526, corresponding to fols. 247r-256v.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and 628 JPEG derivatives at 1600-pixel width

entities.label: Bannatyne manuscript, NLS Adv.MS.1.1.6
entities.description: George Bannatyne's 1568 anthology. Orpheus occupies volume II, fols. 317v-325r (legacy pages 684-699) and preserves the complete 633-line version ending ‘Finis: quod mr. R. H.’

holding.notes: The Orpheus passage is in present physical volume II.

physical_description.extent: 375 folios in the catalog description; Orpheus at fols. 317v-325r
physical_description.format: Poetry anthology now bound in two physical volumes
notes: The main manuscript's later pagination 684-699 supplies an additional locator for the work.

entities.label: NLS complete digital surrogate of Bannatyne manuscript volume II
entities.description: The complete 454-canvas IIIF object for present physical volume II. Orpheus occupies canvases 318-333, corresponding to fols. 317v-325r.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and 454 JPEG derivatives at 1600-pixel width

entities.label: Robert L. Kindrick, ed., The Poems of Robert Henryson (1997)
entities.description: A Middle English Texts Series edition published by Medieval Institute Publications. Its Orpheus text uses Bannatyne as the complete base while recording and adopting readings from Asloan and Chepman-Myllar. It is a later comparison edition, not an upstream source of the 1993 encoding.

bibliographic.citation: The Poems of Robert Henryson, ed. Robert L. Kindrick (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 1997).
bibliographic.title: The Poems of Robert Henryson
bibliographic.pages: Orpheus introduction, text, and notes pp. 186-221; text pp. 192-209

entities.label: METS downloadable scan of Kindrick's 1997 edition
entities.description: A complete 153-page, two-up scanned PDF distributed from the official METS edition. The Orpheus introduction, text, and notes occupy PDF pages 96-113.

scope.notes: Matching identifier, header, structure, and text establish derivation; the exact repository transfer event is undocumented.

subject.description: 633-line mixed-case U-M XML text with supplied structural markup

scope.notes: After COCOA/XML markup and supplied headings are removed, both stages contain 633 verse lines and 632 normalized lines match exactly; only line 98 changes from OTA BUS to U-M buss. The OTA header records that the text formerly belonged to folder 0243, accounting for U-M's U*-243-A reference. The precise transfer event is not documented.

The OTA header explicitly cites Smith volume III pp. 26-64, identifies the Chepman-Myllar text as the apparent base, and warns that variants may be included. Comparison shows Asloan-dominant supply for the print copy's lost lines 59-175, with local Bannatyne or hybrid readings, and Bannatyne-only additions at 509-514, 547-550, and 571-615. The exact rule governing local variant choice remains undocumented.

subject.description: PB milestones in the electronic text

scope.notes: The 22 PB milestones reproduce Smith pages 66-87 exactly; this scoped edge does not claim those pages supplied every reading.

object.description: 461 surviving lines of the imperfect unique Orpheus copy
locators: lost first-gathering leaves formerly carrying unified lines 59-175

scope.notes: Smith explicitly labels A* as the Biilbring transcript; this preserves the intermediary rather than falsely asserting direct Smith-to-codex transcription.
Smith says the transcript was made when the manuscript was deposited at the British Museum.

object.description: Smith volume III as reproduced by this derivative, omitting at least printed page 37

scope.notes: The cached derivative and its OCR jump directly from printed page 36 to 38. A separate Google Books page image supplies printed page 37 for source verification.
The viewer's full page sequence includes printed page 37 and exposes a full-PDF route. Only page 37 is locally cached because direct full-PDF retrieval required an interactive challenge.

subject.description: Images 149-166 within the complete surviving 220-image published sequence

object.description: Surviving Orpheus title and text leaves, with the two-leaf lacuna preserved rather than silently repaired

scope.notes: Image 152 ends before the lost material and image 153 resumes at unified line 176. Image 166 finishes Orpheus and then begins the separate Want of Wise Men on the same surface.
The Orpheus portion is fols. 247r-256v / canvases 507-526.

subject.description: Complete 454-canvas digital surrogate of present volume II

object.description: Present physical volume II of the Bannatyne manuscript

scope.notes: The Orpheus portion is fols. 317v-325r / canvases 318-333.
Kindrick uses Bannatyne for completeness but supplies editorial normalization and readings from the other witnesses; transcribes does not mean diplomatic reproduction.

local_copies.label: Chepman and Myllar Prints complete 220-image published sequence

work_portion.label: Surviving leaves of the imperfect unique Chepman-Myllar Orpheus tract
notes: Image 150 is the title verso and bears a manuscript ‘Orpheus’; printed poem text begins on image 151.
Image 152 ends at line 58; image 153 resumes at unified line 176 because the third and fourth leaves of the first gathering, which carried lines 59-175, are lost from the unique copy.
Image 166 ends ‘And thus endis the tale of Orpheus’ and then begins the separate Want of Wise Men on the same surface.
Complete here means all 220 published facsimile images in this derivative, not a materially complete copy of the Orpheus tract or unrepresented physical covers and flyleaves.

rights.basis: The work was created and transmitted centuries before modern copyright terms.
notes: This conclusion does not automatically transfer to modern editions, encodings, scans, metadata, or provider terms.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Three early Orpheus versions of different lengths and readings
editorial_practices.representation: Separately printed Chepman-Myllar, Asloan-transcript, and Bannatyne streams
editorial_practices.description: Smith preserves the three streams separately and uses cross-reference notes; the printed streams have 461 surviving, 578, and 633 lines. The first number reflects two leaves lost from the unique Chepman-Myllar copy, not an intentionally shorter complete print text.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The 1984 OSTA/OTA uppercase COCOA payload, together with Smith's Bannatyne-stream pagination
editorial_practices.representation: One structured mixed-case 633-line XML item with two conceptual parts, six supplied navigation subdivisions, and page milestones 66-87; front matter, back matter, and notes omitted
editorial_practices.description: After headings and markup are removed, all 633 U-M verse lines align with the OTA payload and 632 match under case and non-alphanumeric normalization; line 98 changes OTA BUS to U-M buss. U-M adds division, line-group, navigation, and page structures while preserving the earlier payload's continuous reading stream.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Smith's separate Chepman-Myllar, Asloan-transcript, and Bannatyne streams
editorial_practices.representation: A single continuous reading text rather than Smith's parallel streams
editorial_practices.description: The available OTA text is Chepman-dominant, fills the print copy's lost lines 59-175 mainly from Asloan, includes Bannatyne-only additions at 509-514, 547-550, and 571-615, and retains local Bannatyne or hybrid readings. The exact 1984 rule governing every variant choice remains undocumented.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Bannatyne's complete text with readings from Asloan and Chepman-Myllar
editorial_practices.representation: A modern reading edition based on Bannatyne and documented through textual notes
editorial_practices.description: Kindrick's notes repeatedly identify readings supplied or preferred from the two shorter witnesses; the edition remains collateral to the older electronic chain.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item HenOrpheus.
evidence.summary: Confirms public item identity, title, author, table of contents, electronic publication details, source statement, and No Copyright-United States rights statement.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, legacy item record 2231, [Orpheus and Eurydice].
evidence.summary: Confirms public item identity, exact header and payload filenames, A. J. Aitken, P. Bratley, and N. Hamilton-Smith as machine-readable-version creators, Harry D. Watson and DOST as depositors, the 1984-05-01 deposit date, Smith volume III as source, and CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 licensing.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive item 2231 TEI catalog header, header2231.xml.
evidence.locator: titleStmt; publicationStmt; sourceDesc; encodingDesc; profileDesc; revisionDesc
evidence.summary: The valid fuller legacy header names the machine-readable creators, depositors, OSTA/DOST purpose, 1984 deposit, Smith volume III pp. 26-64 source, apparent Chepman-Myllar base with possible variants, COCOA and character-normalization practices, CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 license, former folder 0243, and the separate unavailable orpheus2.2231 copy lacking lines 59-175. The current migrated direct header is shorter and malformed, so it is retained only as an alternate URL.

evidence.citation: Current LLDS migration derivative of Oxford Text Archive item 2231 header2231.xml.
evidence.locator: publicationStmt, especially the unmatched authority and distributor tags
evidence.summary: Fixity and XML parsing establish that the current 8,958-byte migration derivative differs from the valid 9,712-byte legacy header: it is not well-formed XML and omits the deposit date, distributor block, duplicate OTA identifier, and PURL. It is retained separately so the current endpoint is represented without overwriting the fuller legacy evidence.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive item 2231 complete available Orpheus payload, orpheus1-2231.txt.
evidence.locator: title and source headings; COCOA tags; complete verse payload
evidence.summary: Verifies the available uppercase COCOA source file, sparse page and folio tags, and all 633 verse lines from the tale through Moralitas.

evidence.citation: Reproducible line-level comparison of OTA orpheus1-2231.txt with General Corpus HenOrpheus.xml.
evidence.locator: OTA verse payload and XML L elements N=1-633
evidence.summary: After OTA headings and COCOA tags are removed, both sources yield 633 aligned verse lines. Case-folded alphanumeric normalization matches 632 lines exactly; only line 98 differs, with OTA BUS and U-M buss. The XML also converts case, expands structural markup, and adds supplied navigation divisions and Smith page milestones.

evidence.citation: Line-level comparison of OTA orpheus1-2231.txt with Smith's three parallel 1908 Orpheus streams.
evidence.locator: OTA lines 1-633 compared with Smith printed pp. 26-87
evidence.summary: Establishes a Chepman-dominant continuous text, Asloan-dominant reconstruction of the unique print copy's lost lines 59-175, Bannatyne-only additions at 509-514, 547-550, and 571-615, and local Bannatyne or hybrid readings including lines 71, 94, 616, 623, and 630. The exact OSTA choice rule remains undocumented.

evidence.citation: G. Gregory Smith, ed., The Poems of Robert Henryson, vol. III, Text vol. II, Scottish Text Society 58 (Edinburgh and London: William Blackwood and Sons, 1908).
evidence.locator: title page; prefatory note and tables pp. vii-xvi; Orpheus pp. 26-87 except printed p. 37
evidence.summary: The qpdf-clean 232-page Internet Archive derivative verifies the bibliography, Stevenson and Biilbring acknowledgments, separately printed streams, their 461-surviving/578/633-line extents, source labels, and most relevant readings and pagination. It jumps from printed p. 36 to 38; the separate Google Books p. 37 image completes the reviewed Orpheus range.

evidence.citation: Google Books full-view scan of Smith volume III, printed page 37.
evidence.summary: Visually verifies the Asloan transcript material absent from the Internet Archive derivative and establishes that Google Books volume gtgKYJ8JMAgC provides a separate full-view scan. Only this exact gap-closing page image, not the complete Google volume, is locally cached.

evidence.summary: Verifies the three-text census, current and historical loci, Biilbring transcript intermediary, version lengths, R. H. subscription and attribution evidence, and Boethius-Trivet source discussion.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for poemsofroberthen01henr.
evidence.summary: Supplies exact public filenames, file inventory, item identity, and the absence of an explicit item-level reuse license.

evidence.citation: Source-chain comparison among OTA item 2231, HenOrpheus.xml, and Smith volume III's three Orpheus streams.
evidence.locator: XML lines 1-633 and PB 66-87 compared with Smith pp. 26-87
evidence.summary: Confirms that U-M preserves the 1984 OTA payload's continuous reading stream while adding Smith's Bannatyne-stream PB milestones 66-87. Comparison with Smith locates Chepman-dominant readings, the reconstructed print lacuna, and Bannatyne additions at the OTA stage rather than attributing undocumented collation work to U-M.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, First Scottish Books: Chepman and Myllar prints.
evidence.summary: Identifies the unique 1508 Edinburgh print collection, printers, tract sequence, NLS holding context, and official image viewer.

evidence.citation: Wikimedia Commons, File:Chepman and Myllar.djvu.
evidence.summary: Supplies the exact complete 220-image public DjVu derivative and its public-domain file-page assertion.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of the complete Chepman and Myllar DjVu, images 149-168.
evidence.locator: title image 149; poem images 151-166; following ballad images 166-168
evidence.summary: Locates the title and 461 surviving Orpheus lines at images 149-166, identifies image 150 as the verso, observes the break from line 58 on image 152 to unified line 176 on image 153 caused by two lost leaves, verifies the poem's end at the top of image 166, and separates The Want of Wise Men beginning later on that surface.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland manuscripts catalogue, MS 16500, Asloan manuscript.
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, date range, John Asloan's role, physical extent, holding, contents, and linked complete digital object.

evidence.citation: NLS IIIF Presentation manifest for the Asloan manuscript, digital object 235163104.
evidence.summary: Verifies the complete surviving-manuscript digital boundary, canvas sequence, exact image services, Public Domain Mark, and Orpheus range.

evidence.citation: Local integrity and work-range audit of the complete Asloan NLS IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: Verified the 628-item inventory and complete bundle inherited from the shared source object, then visually fixed Orpheus at canvases 507-526. Start and end image hashes and direct URLs were recorded, with fols. 247r-256v reconciled to the canvas sequence.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland manuscripts catalogue, Adv.MS.1.1.6, Bannatyne manuscript.
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, 1568 compilation, George Bannatyne's role, codex contents and structure, physical description, present two-volume arrangement, and linked digital objects.

evidence.citation: NLS IIIF Presentation manifest for Bannatyne manuscript volume II, digital object 232641636.
evidence.locator: 454 canvases; Orpheus canvases 318-333 / fols. 317v-325r / legacy pp. 684-699
evidence.summary: Verifies the complete present-volume digital boundary, canvas sequence, exact image services, Public Domain Mark, and Orpheus range.

evidence.citation: Local integrity and work-range audit of the complete Bannatyne volume II NLS IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: Verified the 454-item inventory and complete bundle inherited from the shared source object, then visually fixed Orpheus at canvases 318-333. The opening and closing surfaces are shared with adjacent works; start and end image hashes and direct URLs were recorded.

evidence.citation: Catalogue of English Literary Manuscripts 1450-1700, Robert Henryson author entries and introduction.
evidence.locator: HnR14, Bannatyne ff. 317v-325r; HnR15, Asloan ff. 247r-256v
evidence.summary: Supports accepted Henryson authorship, the 633-line Bannatyne and 578-line Asloan loci, the 1508 first publication, and current scholarly witness identifiers.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, current General Introduction to The Complete Works of Robert Henryson.
evidence.summary: Provides cautious Henryson biography and Older Scots context, Chepman-Myllar April 1508 print details, Asloan and Bannatyne dates and loci, textual completeness, and the separate status of The Want of Wise Men.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series edition landing page for The Poems of Robert Henryson, ed. Robert L. Kindrick.
evidence.summary: Confirms edition identity, editor, publisher, 1997 publication, public web reading routes, and official downloadable PDF.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, Kindrick edition, Orpheus and Eurydice introduction, text, and notes.
evidence.summary: Supports romance and mythographic interpretation, Boethius and Nicholas Trivet source relationships, Bannatyne base-text choice, and documented comparison with Asloan and Chepman-Myllar readings.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection and page-range audit of the complete official METS PDF of Kindrick's 1997 edition.
evidence.locator: title and copyright pages; PDF pp. 96-113 / printed pp. 186-221
evidence.summary: Verifies the complete 153-page PDF boundary, Robert L. Kindrick's editorship, Medieval Institute Publications' 1997 imprint, ISBN 1-879288-94-X, © 1997 Board of the Medieval Institute statement, and exact Orpheus introduction/text/notes range.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland copyright and reuse guidance for digital material.
evidence.summary: States that faithful digitizations have the same copyright status as the originals and that NLS waives residual digitization-layer rights; supports separate treatment of historical objects, digitizations, metadata, and site content.

open_questions.question: What exact OSTA/DOST editorial rule governed choices among Smith's three streams in the available orpheus1.2231 payload, especially its local hybrid readings?
next_steps: Seek OSTA/DOST operator instructions or production notes and complete a variant concordance across the OTA payload and all three Smith streams.
notes: The direct 1984 OTA-to-1993 U-M chain and broad source pattern are resolved: Chepman dominates, Asloan mainly supplies the lost print leaves, and Bannatyne supplies its unique additions. Only the rule for local variant choices remains open.

open_questions.question: Does Professor Biilbring's working transcript of the Asloan Orpheus survive, and if so under what shelfmark or archival description?
next_steps: Search Smith and Biilbring papers, Scottish Text Society archives, Bonn holdings, and NLS acquisition files before assigning a location or declaring the transcript lost.

open_questions.question: Does the Asloan Orpheus depend directly on the 1508 Chepman-Myllar print?
next_steps: Review the stemmatic argument and variants in current textual scholarship; do not add a direct derivation edge while the relationship remains only a possibility.

open_questions.question: What is the current precise NLS shelfmark for the mounted Chepman and Myllar print collection formerly designated Advocates Library 19.1.16?
next_steps: Ask NLS rare-books staff or consult the current rare-books catalog before citing a present shelfmark.

review_notes: The intellectual work includes both the 414-line tale and 219-line Moralitas. The Want of Wise Men is a separate work despite its placement and advertisement in the 1508 tract.

Chepman-Myllar, Asloan, and Bannatyne are three textual streams with 461 surviving, 578, and 633 lines. The unique Chepman-Myllar copy has lost the two leaves that carried unified lines 59-175; its surviving count is not modeled as an intentionally shorter complete version.
Professor Biilbring's transcript is retained as an explicit intermediary between Asloan and Smith; Smith is not falsely linked as a direct transcriber of the inaccessible codex.

The source chain is explicit: the repository XML copies U-M's 1993 U*-243-A item, which transforms the available 1984 OSTA/OTA item 2231 payload, which in turn cites and combines Smith's separately printed streams. The OTA and U-M stages align for all 633 verse lines, with 632 exact normalized matches and only BUS versus buss at line 98.

Sixteen exact source files are cached: complete 220-image Chepman, 628-canvas Asloan, and 454-canvas Bannatyne bundles; Smith volume I; the incomplete Internet Archive Smith volume III derivative plus its missing printed page 37 from Google Books; separate legacy and current-migration OTA headers, the complete available OTA payload; and the complete METS PDF. Exact work ranges, shared boundary surfaces, working direct image links, byte counts, and hashes remain visible.
Boethius and Nicholas Trivet are intellectual sources modeled in the descriptive metadata. No surviving physical exemplar is invented in the lineage graph.
Kindrick 1997 is a later collateral comparison edition. Its official PDF is locally cached for research but remains copyrighted and is not an upstream source of the 1993 encoding.

Access, local caching, underlying-work copyright, edition copyright, image rights statements, and provider terms are separate assertions. A downloaded file is not treated as automatically redistributable.
